channel.title: Open Source on Rafelia's AI LOLs
channel.description: Recent content in Open Source on Rafelia's AI LOLs

item.title: 🧩 Dapr Goes Agent Mode! Now Supporting AI Agents Like a Pro 🤖🔗
item.description:
🧩 Dapr Goes Agent Mode! Now Supporting AI Agents Like a Pro 🤖🔗

🎉 Plot twist! Microsoft’s open-source microservices runtime, Dapr, just unlocked AI agent mode! Yup, Dapr can now help you build smarter, faster, and more scalable AI agents—no cap. 🚀

item.title: ⚡ Google-Backed AI Partnership Launches with $400M for Public Interest AI! 🤖💰
item.description: Current AI launches with $400M to fund ethical AI for healthcare, climate action, and transparency—backed by Europe, Global South, and tech giants like Google & Salesforce.

item.title: 🚀 DeepSeek-R1: 6 Wild AI Use Cases Taking the Community by Storm! 🌪️
item.description:
🤯 DeepSeek-R1 is Taking Over the AI Scene!

Move over, OpenAI—there’s a new AI superstar in town! 🇨🇳 DeepSeek-R1, the powerful (and super affordable) reasoning model, is stealing the spotlight from OpenAI, Meta, and Microsoft.

item.title: 💡 Sky-T1: Open Source AI That Thinks (For Only $450!)
item.description:
Sky-T1: The $450 Reasoning AI That’s Making Waves

🚀 On Friday, UC Berkeley’s Sky Computing Lab dropped Sky-T1-32B-Preview, an open source reasoning model that can compete with earlier versions of OpenAI’s o1 on key benchmarks. Here’s the kicker: it was trained for less than $450! 💸

What’s the Big Deal?

💻 Most AI models cost millions to train, but Sky-T1 shows you can replicate high-level reasoning capabilities without robbing a bank. 🏦 It’s basically the IKEA of AI – affordable, efficient, and you might need a manual to assemble it. 🛠️🤖
